title: FCC lets Amazon Leo miss deployment deadline with temporary spectrum penalty
summary: The FCC has granted Amazon's Project Kuiper a temporary reprieve from its deployment deadline for its broadband satellite constellation, but with a spectrum penalty that may benefit competitors like SpaceX. Amazon can now miss the July 30 deadline for deploying half of its planned 3,232 satellites.

title: Russia is jamming GPS from space
summary: Russia is reportedly jamming GPS signals from space, posing a significant risk to America's GPS systems. This development was highlighted in a SpaceNews opinion article in October 2024.

title: Quantum Space to go public in SPAC deal
summary: Quantum Space, a company led by a former NASA administrator, will go public by merging with a SPAC. The company focuses on developing highly maneuverable spacecraft for national security missions.

title: WRC-27: the next arena for U.S.-China space competition
summary: China filed for 200,000 additional satellites, escalating competition with the U.S. ahead of the WRC-27 event. This move highlights China's aggressive push to dominate the growing space economy.

title: Axiom and Prada advance design of spacesuit
summary: Axiom Space, in partnership with Prada, has unveiled the design of a lunar spacesuit element for NASA. The collaboration aims to enhance the functionality and aesthetics of the spacesuit.

title: Speed as a Strategic Advantage in Border Monitoring
summary: Europe faces rapidly changing border environments requiring real-time monitoring. Space-based solutions are highlighted as a strategic advantage for tracking dynamic activities.

title: UK startup NewOrbit raises $18.5 million in Series A round
summary: UK startup NewOrbit Space raised $18.5 million in a Series A funding round to develop satellites for very low Earth orbit (VLEO). The investment will support the company's growth in the emerging VLEO satellite market.

title: OQ Technology plans direct-to-smartphone demo with cellular spectrum
summary: OQ Technology plans to demonstrate direct-to-smartphone satellite connectivity in Germany next year using a local telco's cellular spectrum, aiming to compete with U.S.-led services in the emerging market.

title: NASA to select new headquarters building by end of year
summary: NASA intends to choose a new headquarters building by the end of the year, though it will stay within the Washington area. The decision is part of ongoing efforts to modernize its operational facilities.

title: Rounding up the space unicorns
summary: Billion-dollar space startups, once rare, are now increasing in number across emerging orbital markets. This trend highlights the growing commercialization and investment in the space industry.

title: Scientists propose spraying chemicals into Earth's magnetic field to protect us from powerful solar storms
summary: Scientists have proposed a spacecraft-based system to actively strengthen Earth's magnetic shield by spraying chemicals into the magnetic field. This approach aims to reduce the impact of powerful solar storms on our planet.

title: James Webb Space Telescope weighs 'sleeping giant' black hole from 10 billion light-years away — and it's 6 billion times our sun's mass
summary: Astronomers used the James Webb Space Telescope to measure a dormant supermassive black hole 10 billion light-years away, which has a mass 6 billion times that of the Sun. The measurement was aided by gravitational lensing, a phenomenon predicted by Einstein.

title: Why do Venus and Jupiter meet up in the sky so often? It's a symptom of a solar system that supports life on Earth
summary: Venus and Jupiter frequently align in the sky due to the solar system's orbital dynamics, which may also contribute to conditions supporting life on Earth.

title: This former Paralympic athlete could become the 1st person with a physical disability to live in orbit
summary: European Space Agency astronaut John McFall, a former Paralympic athlete, may become the first person with a physical disability to live in orbit. He could be headed to the private Haven-1 space station as early as 2027.

title: Watch SpaceX set new rocket reuse record with 35th launch — and landing — of a Falcon 9 booster (video)
summary: SpaceX achieved a new rocket reuse record by launching and landing a Falcon 9 booster for its 35th time. The mission carried 29 Starlink satellites from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station.

title: Get ready for the next Pentagon file drop with our essential UFO reading list
summary: The Pentagon is expected to release another batch of declassified UFO-related files soon. A list of 15 books is provided to explore the history, impact, and possible explanations of UFOs.

title: Astronaut spots Mount Vesuvius from space | Space photo of the day for June 8, 2026
summary: An astronaut captured a stunning space photo of Mount Vesuvius from orbit on June 8, 2026. The image highlights the volcano's beauty as seen from space.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: The upcoming week will see launches from China, Rocket Lab, and Starlink, including three Starlink missions. Flights are also scheduled from California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the recent failure of its New Glenn rocket during hotfire testing. The company is focusing on future objectives following the loss at Launch Complex 36.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability and future missions.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 is assessing damage caused by an anomaly and identifying future risk areas. The Eastern Range operations are being evaluated for potential impacts.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key logistical step for the mission, though timelines and landers remain uncertain.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36, aiming for a return-to-flight (RTF) by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week. Five missions are scheduled, with SpaceX and Chinese organizations leading the launches.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. This discovery enhances our understanding of early cosmic structures.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a major anomaly during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36, resulting in an explosion. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the event, company, or category were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: ESA will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Researchers in ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section, including many young minds, test and develop materials to ensure spacecraft survive the harsh conditions of space. Their work supports the success of ESA's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A test involving the baking of parachutes for a space mission to ensure proper deployment and functionality under extreme conditions. This process is crucial for validating the safety and reliability of landing systems.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling precise and secure handling of objects in orbit. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Quantum Space To Go Public Via SPAC
summary: Quantum Space announced plans to go public via a SPAC merger to secure capital for scaling and access to public markets. The company emphasized the need for speed in its strategic move.

title: NewOrbit Raises $18.5M to Build VLEO Spacecraft at Scale
summary: NewOrbit has raised $18.5 million to develop and scale VLEO-capable spacecraft. The company plans to launch its first VLEO satellite, NEO-1, in 2028.

title: Juno Propulsion Raises $1.4M Pre-Seed for RDE Flight
summary: Juno Propulsion secured $1.4 million in pre-seed funding to advance its first flight-ready rotating detonation engine (RDE), named Project Iris, with a demo mission planned for 2027.

title: Mass Is the Easy Part, Coordination Is The Hard Part
summary: Ukraine's use of cheap drones has forced the U.S. to adopt mass-produced, autonomous, and disposable systems to counter adversaries. The shift emphasizes coordination and quantity over expensive platforms in modern conflict.

title: AstroForge Completes its DeepSpace-2 Spacecraft
summary: AstroForge has completed its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft, which aims to demonstrate the company's ability to rendezvous with an asteroid. This mission sets the foundation for future asteroid mining efforts planned before the end of the decade.

title: Apex Raises $200M At $2.3B Valuation
summary: Apex has raised $200 million in a funding round, valuing the company at $2.3 billion. This is the third such round in approximately 14 months.

title: US Commercial Space Stations Expand to Europe
summary: The U.S. is expanding its commercial space station initiatives to include Europe, ensuring continued access to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) as the International Space Station (ISS) nears retirement. This move highlights growing international collaboration in commercial space ventures.

title: Recapping Planet’s Q1 With CFO and President Ashley Johnson
summary: Planet's Q1 recap highlights its differentiation in revenue recognition compared to hardware-focused companies. The discussion with CFO and President Ashley Johnson emphasizes Planet's unique approach to financial performance.

title: The Exploration Company Completes Nyx Drop Test
summary: The Exploration Company successfully completed a drop test for its Nyx capsule on May 19 to evaluate parachute performance and landing dynamics. The test used a dedicated drop-test vehicle to assess controlled descent and landing.

title: Austria’s First Commercial Sat is Ready for Launch
summary: Austria's first commercial satellite, Oasis Alpha, has been shipped by Vienna-based startup Tumbleweed to launch integrator Exolaunch for a planned July launch. The satellite is part of Austria's growing space industry ambitions.

title: Quantum Space to Go Public in SPAC Deal
summary: Quantum Space, led by former NASA Administrator Jim Bridenstine, plans to go public via a SPAC merger with Inflection Point Acquisition. The deal values Quantum Space at an undisclosed amount.

title: Finland’s Voimatel to Integrate Eutelsat LEO for Arctic Communications
summary: Voimatel will integrate Eutelsat's Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) connectivity to enhance Arctic communications. The Finnish company specializes in fixed and wireless communication networks.

title: KSAT to Lead European Pollution-Focused Poseidon EO Mission
summary: KSAT will lead the European Poseidon Earth observation mission aimed at tracking pollution using optical and radar satellite technology. The project was announced under a new award.

title: FCC Kicks Off Review of Amazon/Globalstar Deal
summary: The FCC has initiated a review process for Amazon's acquisition of Globalstar, with comments due by July 6. The deal was announced by Amazon in April.

title: Inside the State of the Satellite Industry 2026 Report with SIA President Tom Stroup
summary: The article discusses the 'State of the Satellite Industry 2026 Report' and features insights from SIA President Tom Stroup, highlighting industry projections and demand for satellite broadband and direct-to-device services.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo 50% Deployment Waiver, With Conditions on Spectrum Priority
summary: The FCC granted Amazon a 50% deployment waiver for its Project Kuiper constellation by the end of July, but imposed conditions on spectrum priority. This decision impacts Amazon's satellite internet plans.

title: Apex Hits $2.3B Valuation With Latest Funding Raise
summary: Apex Space, a satellite bus manufacturer, raised an additional $200 million in capital, bringing its valuation to $2.3 billion. This funding round more than doubles the company's previous valuation.

title: Aalyria to Support Orchestration for NASA SCaN Program
summary: NASA has selected Aalyria to support orchestration for its Space Communications and Navigation (SCaN) Program, starting with the PExT demonstration mission. The PExT mission, Polylingual Experimental Terminal, aims to advance communication technologies in space.

title: Tototheo Global Taps Eutelsat for LEO Service for Maritime
summary: Tototheo Global has partnered with Eutelsat to provide Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) services for maritime applications. This deal expands Eutelsat's reach in the maritime sector through Tototheo Global's distribution network.

title: NGA Awards Planet $22M Luno B Option for Maritime Domain Awareness
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) awarded Planet Labs a $22 million option for maritime domain awareness using AI-enabled analytics. This extends a contract initiated last year to support U.S. Combatant Commands.
